citation.description: The 'Codex Gerrit workflow rule' block at lines 36–43 instructs checking configured identity's 'feature-branch-driver' rights and, when unavailable, using 'git push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main' — exactly the credential-aware fallback.

criteria.criterion: Codex guidance names the credential-aware refs/for/main fallback.

citation.description: Line 39 names 'ticket dependency order'; lines 40–41 name both 'LLM-Review +1' and 'Verified +1' as gates before Submit.

criteria.criterion: The guidance preserves both required Gerrit votes and dependency ordering.

citation.description: Lines 41–43 state 'Authorized drivers may still use the server-side feature-branch flow; see ADR 0025, including its post-ADR-0047 note that the Rebase-If-Necessary interaction needs human review.' Feature branches remain valid for authorized users; the post-cutover caveat is explicitly cited.

criteria.criterion: The guidance cites the post-cutover feature-branch caveat rather than claiming all feature branches are obsolete.

citation.description: test_codex_guidance_has_credential_aware_gerrit_fallback() asserts that specific strings ('recent merged Gerrit history', 'feature-branch-driver', 'git push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main', 'dependency order', 'LLM-Review +1', 'Verified +1', 'ADR 0025', 'not an implementation blocker') all exist in AGENTS.md's Codex Gerrit rule block, preventing accidental removal.

criteria.criterion: A documentation guard prevents accidental removal of the fallback.
